file_insights.changes_summary: Added 6 new model classes: Branch, Role, Permission, Session, UserRole, RolePermission with comprehensive docstrings and field definitions
key_additions: Branch model: Multi-location support with address, coordinates, timezone, operating hours (TimeStampedMixin, SoftDeleteMixin)
Role model: RBAC with platform and organization scopes, system role flag, M2M relationship to Permission
Permission model: Granular resource.action pattern permissions (view, create, update, delete)
Session model: JWT refresh token management with user agent, IP address, expiration tracking
UserRole model: M2M junction with role and organization scope resolution
RolePermission model: M2M junction between Role and Permission with conditional enforcement

patterns_discovered.description: All models follow the same structure: UUID primary key, TimeStampedMixin (created_at, updated_at), SoftDeleteMixin where appropriate, comprehensive docstrings with usage examples
Models use TimeStampedMixin and SoftDeleteMixin for DRY code. Branch and Session include SoftDeleteMixin for soft deletion capability
Branch, Role, and UserRole models enforce tenant isolation through organization FK relationships with CASCADE delete
Branch model uses SoftDeleteManager (excludes soft-deleted) and all_objects Manager (includes all records)
All fields include verbose_name and help_text for Django admin usability and i18n support using gettext_lazy
Models include db_index on frequently filtered fields (status, scope) and composite indexes for common query patterns
Branch model uses JSONField with default=dict for settings and operating_hours, allowing schema flexibility
Role model supports both PLATFORM and ORGANIZATION scopes, enabling hierarchical permission management
Models include @property methods for derived values (is_active, full_address, code) without database storage

gotchas_discovered.gotcha: Unique constraint on (organization, slug) for Branch
gotchas_discovered.impact: Branch slug must be unique within an organization, but same slug can exist across organizations
gotchas_discovered.mitigation: Enforce slug generation to be organization-aware during creation

gotchas_discovered.gotcha: Role.organization can be NULL for platform roles
gotchas_discovered.impact: Query logic must handle null organization field; platform roles are organization-agnostic
gotchas_discovered.mitigation: Always filter by scope (PLATFORM vs ORGANIZATION) before querying organization field

gotchas_discovered.gotcha: Permission.is_system prevents deletion of system permissions
gotchas_discovered.impact: Documentation only; no database-level constraint enforces this - must be enforced at application level
gotchas_discovered.mitigation: Implement permission delete() override to check is_system flag

gotchas_discovered.gotcha: Session.refresh_token is 'hashed' but field definition unclear
gotchas_discovered.impact: Docstring mentions 'Hashed refresh token' but implementation detail not shown in diff; unclear if hash function is used
gotchas_discovered.mitigation: Verify token hashing strategy in Session.save() override or middleware

gotchas_discovered.gotcha: Branch.operating_hours JSONField has no schema validation
gotchas_discovered.impact: Any JSON structure accepted; no validation of day/time format
gotchas_discovered.mitigation: Document expected JSON structure and implement custom validation method

gotchas_discovered.gotcha: UserRole M2M relationship requires explicit through model
gotchas_discovered.impact: Cannot use implicit M2M; through='UserRole' adds complexity
gotchas_discovered.mitigation: Ensure UserRole model is fully defined with proper FK relationships to User and Role

approach_outcome.task_completion: All 6 required models (Branch, Role, Permission, Session, UserRole, RolePermission) successfully created
details: Models include comprehensive docstrings with usage examples
Proper use of Django mixins (TimeStampedMixin, SoftDeleteMixin)
Strategic indexing on frequently queried fields
Proper FK relationships with CASCADE delete for data integrity
Support for multi-tenancy via organization FK
RBAC implementation with platform and organization scopes
All fields properly documented with verbose_name and help_text
approach_outcome.git_commit: 3a96d1e auto-claude: subtask-2-5 - Create Branch, Role, Permission, Session, UserRole, RolePermission models

recommendations.recommendation: Implement database constraints for Permission.is_system and Role.is_system flags via model save() override to prevent unauthorized modifications
Document and implement Session.refresh_token hashing strategy (likely using Django's make_password or similar) for security compliance
Add JSONField validators for Branch.operating_hours to enforce expected structure (e.g., {day: {open: time, close: time}})
Add select_related/prefetch_related documentation for common queries (e.g., Role queries should prefetch permissions via RolePermission)
Document the tenant isolation strategy - all queries should filter by organization except platform-scoped roles
Implement ModelAdmin classes with filters, search, and readonly fields for Branch, Role, Permission, and Session models
Consider moving RBAC-related models (Role, Permission, RolePermission, UserRole) to a separate models module (e.g., rbac.py) for better organization
Create model factories using factory_boy for test coverage of all 6 models with various configurations
